findings.message: New database query path has no observability: no metric recording, span, or logging around the query. The database query `session.scalars(select(Item).where(Item.name == name)).first()` executes without latency instrumentation or error correlation context.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the database query with observability instrumentation. Add a tracer span, record query latency metrics, and ensure errors are logged with correlation ID. Consider using a decorator or context manager to instrument all repository functions consistently.

findings.message: Unbounded query without row-count or latency metric: `session.scalars()` can return a large result set. No metric is recorded for query latency or rows examined before `.first()` terminates the scan.
findings.suggestion: Add instrumentation to record query latency and estimated row count. For production queries on unindexed columns, add metrics that would alert if query performance degrades.

findings.message: Query on non-indexed column `Item.name`: the migration does not create an index on the `name` column, but `get_item_by_name` queries by name. This will result in full table scans.
findings.suggestion: Create a database migration to add an index on `Item.name`, or add observability metrics to detect when this query performs poorly. If the index cannot be added, add a metric to warn when the query scans more than a threshold number of rows.
